MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ashley Rivkin of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (213) 622-7300, appearing via CourtCall for defendant and moving party Eaton Corporation.
Roger E. Gold of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1339, X-134/415-986-1338, X-136), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Roberto Elorreaga and Rosemary Elorreaga.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Eaton Corporation's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Punitive damages is GRANTED.

Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855. Plaintiffs' responses to Defendant's special interrogatories demonstrate that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Defendant's Index of Exhibits, Exs. B and C [Plaintiff Roberto Elorreaga's responses to rogs. 1, 13-25; Exs. D and E [Plaintiff Rosemary Elorreaga's responses to rogs 1, 16-18]; see also Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 96, 107 [where plaintiffs' response to comprehensive interrogatories restate their allegations, or simply provide laundry lists of people and/or documents, the burden of production will almost certainly be shifted].)

Plaintiffs fail to provide a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's objections to portions of the deposition testimony of Walter Zoller are SUSTAINED. (Gold Decl. Ex. C at 55:12-20; 56:17-57:10; 58:20-59:5; 60:19-63:6; 110:17-111:9; 130:14-19; 131:13-25; 141:6-142:6; Exs. 7-10 to the deposition transcript.) Testimony related to Plastics Engineering Company ("Plenco") molding compounds are irrelevant to the issue of punitive damages. Testimony that Defendant received Plenco molding compounds as of 1972 that contained asbestos warnings are irrelevant as Plaintiff's exposure occurred between 1959-1963. The article titled "Safety and Environmental Evaluation of Insulating Media in Medium Voltage Distribution Equipment" is irrelevant as Zoller testified that Cutler Hammer did not make medium-voltage distribution equipment. (Gold Declaration, Ex. C at Ex. 10 to the deposition transcript.) Testimony that Defendant was a member of the National Safety Council, that Cutler-Hammer was first sued in asbestos litigation in 1979, and that Cutler-Hammer was subject to the Walsh-Healey Act are irrelevant as to whether Defendant acted with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others.

Defendant's objection to the declaration of John Templin is SUSTAINED. (Gold Decl. Ex. D.) Templin lacks foundation to opine on whether Defendant had actual knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and his declaration is irrelevant as to whether Defendant acted with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others.

While Plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. The motion was filed on April 8, 2021 and does not comply with the 75 day notice requirement under Code of Civil Procedure section 473c(a)(2). Further, Defendant has not shown that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that Plaintiff Roberto Elorreaga was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant has not provided the Court with a complete set of standard interrogatory questions and responses. (Follett Declaration, Exs. A-B.) Defendant has not demonstrated that it propounded "all facts" special interrogatories. (Follett Declaration, Ex. F.) Even assuming arguendo that it did, Defendant failed to address testimony by Defendant's PMK, William Pratt, identified in Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses. (Follett Declaration, Ex. G at 8.) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
